seoTitle: How to Write Off an Outstanding Invoice in QBO
metaDescription: Learn how to write off an outstanding invoice in QuickBooks Online step by step, useful for uncollectible debts and closed customer accounts.
intro: Not every invoice gets paid, and knowing how to write one off properly keeps your accounts receivable accurate instead of cluttered with debts that will never be collected. This guide covers navigating to the right section of QuickBooks Online, selecting the outstanding invoice, and completing the write-off process. It's aimed at small business owners and accountants managing collections.

steps.heading: Navigate to the correct section
steps.body: Go to the part of QuickBooks Online where outstanding invoices and accounts receivable are managed.

steps.heading: Select the outstanding invoice
steps.body: Identify the specific invoice you need to write off, such as one tied to an uncollectible debt or closed account.

steps.heading: Write off the invoice
steps.body: Complete the write-off process so the invoice no longer shows as outstanding on your books.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for accurate books
steps.body: Keep documentation of why an invoice was written off, and apply consistent practices to avoid future outstanding invoices.

faq.question: When should I write off an invoice in QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Common scenarios include dealing with uncollectible debts or customer accounts that have closed, where the invoice will never realistically be paid.

faq.question: Does writing off an invoice affect my financial reports?
faq.answer: Yes, writing off an invoice updates your accounts receivable, which is why understanding the process is vital for improving cash flow visibility and informed financial decisions.

faq.question: Can I undo a write-off if the invoice gets paid later?
faq.answer: The tutorial covers the standard write-off process; if a written-off invoice is later paid, you'd need to adjust your records accordingly.

faq.question: Is this tutorial useful for both beginners and experienced QuickBooks users?
faq.answer: Yes, it's designed for beginners and seasoned professionals alike, whether you're a small business owner or an accountant.
